data.title: MannKind Non-GAAP EPS of $0.07 beats by $0.01, revenue of $78.35M beats by $2.49M
data.description:
• MannKind press release (NASDAQ:MNKD): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.07 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $78.35M (+18.2% Y/Y) beats by $2.49M.
  • Cash, cash equivalents and investments as of March 31, 2025 totaled $198 million.

data.title: BlackRock TCP Capital Non-GAAP NII of $0.36 beats by $0.02, TII of $55.9M misses by $2.05M
data.description:
• BlackRock TCP Capital press release (NASDAQ:TCPC): Q1 Non-GAAP NII of $0.36 beats by $0.02.
  • Total investment income of $55.9M (+0.3% Y/Y) misses by $2.05M.
  • Net asset value per share was $9.18 as of March 31, 2025 compared to $9.23 as of December 31, 2024.

data.title: Sempra Non-GAAP EPS of $1.44 beats by $0.12, revenue of $3.8B misses by $120M
data.description:
• Sempra press release (NYSE:SRE): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.44 beats by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $3.8B (+4.4% Y/Y) misses by $120M.
  • Sempra is updating its full-year 2025 GAAP earnings-per-common-share (EPS) guidance range to $4.25 – $4.65 vs $4.56 consensus, reflecting actual results through the first quarter, affirming its full-year 2025 adjusted EPS guidance range of $4.30 to $4.70, and affirming its full-year 2026 EPS guidance range of $4.80 to $5.30. The company has also guided to the high-end or above its projected long-term EPS compound annual growth rate of 7% to 9% for 2025 through 2029.

data.title: Novavax GAAP EPS of $2.93 beats by $2.22, revenue of $666.66M beats by $322.81M
data.description:
• Novavax press release (NASDAQ:NVAX): Q1 GAAP EPS of $2.93 beats by $2.22.
  • Revenue of $666.66M (+609.2% Y/Y) beats by $322.81M.
  • Total revenue for the first quarter of 2025 was $667 million, compared to $94 million in the same period in 2024. Higher product sales for the first quarter of 2025 were primarily due to $603 million of revenue recognized with the termination of two Advance Purchase Agreements (APA) and related to cash received in prior years. $45 million of Licensing, Royalties & Other Revenue in the first quarter of 2025 was higher than the prior year due to $40 million of Sanofi revenue associated with upfront payment and milestone amortization and cost reimbursement.

  • Novavax is reiterating Full Year 2025 Financial Guidance for combined R&D and SG&A expenses and currently expects to achieve the following results:

data.title: Cheniere Energy Partners GAAP EPS of $1.08 misses by $0.05, revenue of $2.99B beats by $260M
data.description:
• Cheniere Energy Partners press release (NYSE:CQP): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.08 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $2.99B (+30.0% Y/Y) beats by $260M.
  • Reconfirming full year 2025 distribution guidance of $3.25 - $3.35 per common unit, maintaining a base distribution of $3.10 per common unit.

data.title: Vericel misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q2 and reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Vericel press release (NASDAQ:VCEL): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.23 misses by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $52.6M (+2.5% Y/Y) misses by $1.26M.
  • Non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA for the quarter ended March 31, 2025 was $3.2 million, or 6% of net revenue, compared to $7.2 million, or 14% of net revenue, for the first quarter of 2024. 
  • As of March 31, 2025, the Company had approximately $162 million in cash, restricted cash and investments, and no debt.

  • 2025 Financial Guidance

    • Reaffirmed full-year total revenue guidance with revenue growth expected to be 20% to 23% vs. estimated growth of 20.83% Y/Y
    • Raised full-year profitability guidance to gross margin of 74% and adjusted EBITDA margin of 26%
    • Second quarter total revenue growth expected to be 22% to 25% vs. estimated growth of 23.52% Y/Y

data.title: Warner Bros. in charts: Subscriber base grew ~23% Y/Y in Q1, with a 38% surge overseas
Engie Brasil Energia S.A. reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Engie Brasil Energia S.A. press release (OTCPK:EGIEY): Q1 adjusted net income was R$823M, 3.8% higher than posted in the 1Q24.
  • Net operating revenue reached R$3,013M, 15.5% higher than recorded in 1Q24.

data.title: Bowlero reports Q3 results; not issuing guidance due to economic uncertainty
data.description:
• Bowlero press release (NYSE:LUCK): Q3 Revenue of $339.82M (+0.6% Y/Y) misses by $18.45M.
  • Same Store Revenue decreased 5.6% versus the prior year.
  • Net income of $13.3 million versus prior year net income of $23.8 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $117.3 million versus $122.8 million in the prior year.

  • Guidance

    Due to increasing economic uncertainty, the Company will not be issuing guidance at this time. We intend to reassess our approach to forward-looking guidance later in the year.

    "Although the outlook remains uncertain, we are confident in the Company’s resiliency and our ability to drive revenue growth through strategic initiatives, targeted capital investments, and selective acquisitions," said Bobby Lavan.

data.title: Chimera Investment Q1 earnings improve as book value grows 7.4%
data.description:
Chimera Investment (NYSE:CIM) stock gained 3.7% in Thursday premarket trading after the hybrid mortgage REIT's book value climbed more than analysts expected in Q1 and earnings came in slightly higher than consensus.

Q1 EPS available for distribution of $0.41, topping the average analyst estimate of $0.40, rose from $0.37 in Q4 2024 and $0.37 in last year's Q1.

Besides the improved earnings, "we also made several impactful moves on our balance sheet," said President and CEO Phillip Kardis II. "We successfully accomplished a cash-out refinancing of all the company’s outstanding non-Remic securitizations, which provided approximately $187M in funds for new investment. We also extended two large non-mark to market secured financing facilities until 2027."

GAAP book value of $21.17 per common share at March 31, 2025, climbed from $19.72 at Dec. 31, 2024, beating the consensus estimate of $19.83.

Q1 economic net interest income of $72.3M increased from $69.2M in the prior quarter and $68.0M a year ago.

Q1 economic return improved to 9.2%, compared with 4.40% for full-year 2024.

The REIT increased its provision for credit losses by $3.39M, compared with a $1.35M increase in the year-ago quarter.

Net gains on derivatives were -$2.25M vs. $10.7M a year ago.

The company recorded $8.94M in investment management and advisory fees during the quarter, compared with $0 a year earlier.

Expenses other than interest rose to $34.1M from $22.7M a year ago.

Total assets of $13.2B at March 31, 2025, compared with $13.1B at Dec. 31, 2024.

Earlier, Chimera Investment non-GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.01, revenue of $69.22M misses by $4.53M
